narration: Because both arrays are sorted, the next smallest value must be at one of the two front pointers.
callout: Sorted order makes the merge easy to reason about.

narration: Greedy merge compares the two current front values and takes the smaller one next.
decision: Only the two front values matter at each step.

predictionPrompt.prompt: After taking the smaller front value, what should happen?

choices.label: Move that array's pointer forward
choices.feedback: Correct. The next smallest remaining value will be at one of the current pointers again.

choices.label: Sort both arrays again
choices.feedback: Not this one. The arrays are already sorted.

narration: When one array ends, the leftover values from the other array can be appended directly.
